title: Zeison Sha
content:

{{Quote|If not for self-reliance, our ancestors would have died shortly after your ancestors left them here.|A Zeison Sha warrior, speaking to emissaries of the Jedi Order, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="EGTTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>}}
The '''Zeison Sha''' were an order of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force adepts]] known for their survival skills and self-reliance, as well as their abilities in [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] and with the [[discblade]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Yanibar]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. The Zeison Sha developed their [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] as a means of withstanding their harsh planet. The order stressed independence and survival as well as assistance to those in need. Long estranged from the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], they had little use for the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]] proper.

The Zeison Sha experienced an influx of population when many friends and family of the Jedi, some of whom were Force-sensitive, fled to Yanibar during the [[New Sith Wars]]. For [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]], after those [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] were abandoned on Yanibar due to the deaths of all the Jedi who knew of their location, the Zeison Sha developed an isolationist philosophy. Unlike the Jedi Order, the Zeison Sha did not separate [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive children from their [[Parent/Legends|parents]] for training. Further attempts by the Jedi Order to re-establish relationships with the Zeison Sha ended in failure; the group survived attempts during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] to stamp them out, avoiding [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and other [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunters]]. Even through the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Zeison Sha remained independent and spiteful toward the Jedi.

==Description==
===Philosophy===
{{Quote|Their philosophy is so centered on being able to take care of oneself and one's family, and even I must admit that most Jedi, myself included, cannot comprehend this form of devotion.|Jedi Master Bodo Baas, on the Zeison Sha, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="EGTTF" />}}
[[File:Zeisoninitiatearmor.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The armor of a Zeison Sha initiate]]
Members of the Zeison Sha believed in independence and self-reliance. Originally, the flight to their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Yanibar]] was supposed to be temporary, but during the [[New Sith Wars]], all of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who knew of it perished in battle, leaving the [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] on their own. After their perceived betrayal by the Jedi due to no relief ever reaching Yanibar, the adherents of the ways of the Zeison Sha had no desire to be beholden to anyone.<ref name="EGTTF" /> This mindset allowed them to not only endure Yanibar's inhospitable conditions but prosper and become self-sufficient. Taking care of one's family was a central tenet of their beliefs and was reflected in their usage of [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]]. To the Zeison Sha, [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] was primarily a means to not only defend themselves and their family, but also to aid those who could not defend themselves. As such, they were known to be generous, and aiding others was considered by them to be honorable and noble.<ref name="Hero's Guide">''[[Hero's Guide]]''</ref>

Unlike the strictly regimented ranks of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], the Zeison Sha did not have a rigid organization in their society,<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> although the ranks of initiate and warrior did exist.<ref name="kotorII" /> Children born to [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Parent/Legends|parents]] would be trained at home in an informal manner. For Force-sensitives born to non–Force-sensitive parents, an experienced Force-sensitive adult would train the child, although unlike [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]], the child remained at home to grow as an individual.<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> This was done to foster a normal family upbringing for the child.<ref name="JATM" /> In fact, the Zeison Sha adamantly opposed the idea of separating Force-sensitive children from their parents.<ref name="EGTTF" /> The Zeison Sha did have a temple on Yanibar, the [[Sha Kalan]], where they trained.<ref name="JATM" />

By and large, the isolationism and independence of the Zeison Sha precluded any attempts to seize power across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]; their society was too internally focused. Nevertheless, a smoldering resentment of the Jedi Order coupled with the pride that sometimes arose out of an overly independent mindset led several members of the order to fall prey to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] throughout Zeison Sha history. These aberrants,<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> known as Dark Zeison Sha,<ref name="JATM" /> then had to be hunted down or turned back to prevent them from spreading their influence in the community.<ref name="Hero's Guide" />

===Common abilities===
{{Quote|I had heard that Zeison Sha are masters of the telekinetic powers, but I was astonished to see that their abilities in this area far surpass those of many Jedi.|Jedi Master Bodo Baas, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="EGTTF" />}}
As a Force-based organization, the Zeison Sha specialized in [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetic abilities]], able to perform great feats of skill with minimal effort. These ranged from pushing, pulling, hurling, levitating, smashing, and crushing objects with ease. Their telekinetic ability was, in many ways, superior to that evidenced by many members of the Jedi Order. At higher levels, the use of telekinesis became second nature; a near-instinctual process. With this affinity, members possessed the power to use telekinesis for defense, able to quickly and accurately use rocks and other loose objects to block shots from projectile [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]], or even using the Force itself to create a physical [[Force barrier/Legends|shield]].<ref name="Hero's Guide" />

This was described as the Zeison Sha wrapping the Force around their body to resist damage.<ref name="EGTTF" /> Zeison Sha Masters became so secure in their use of telekinesis that they were able to perform incredible feats of telekinesis even under immense stress and distraction to their concentration, allowing them to use their abilities while experiencing great adversity.<ref name="Hero's Guide" />

[[File:Discblade-HerosGuide.png|thumb|right|250px|A Zeison Sha discblade]]
Zeison Sha warriors served as guardians of the community, with their powers in telekinesis and mastery of the [[discblade]], a razor-edged throwing weapon, garnering them considerable prowess. Trained in its use from a young age, a Zeison Sha could hurl a discblade with great accuracy before calling it back to their hand. Experienced warriors were also known to have their discblades [[Force weapon|imbued]] with the power of the Force.<ref name="Hero's Guide" />

The Zeison Sha also had their own light [[Armor/Legends|armor]] designs, including [[Zeison Sha Initiate armor|Initiate armor]] and the more advanced and protective [[Zeison Sha Warrior armor|Warrior armor]]. The armor afforded them some defensive capability with only a slight reduction in mobility, and typical of the Zeison Sha's independent nature, both models of armor could be customized to suit each wearer's preferences.<ref name="kotorII" />

==History==
===Refuge from the Sith===
Native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Yanibar on the boundary between the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] and [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], the Zeison Sha developed their Force powers as a means of withstanding their harsh environment. The Zeison Sha organization was established<ref name="kotorII" /> by [[3951 BBY]].<ref name="TEA KOTORII date" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
During the [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]], an individual affiliated with one of the factions sent a companion to the Zeison Sha on Yanibar to obtain their support.<ref name="SWTOR">{{TORcite|exp=shadow|type=crewskill|Might of the Zeison Sha}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

[[File:NewSithWars.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The New Sith Wars, which caused many friends and family of the Jedi to flee to Yanibar]]
A major development of Zeison Sha history occurred near the beginning of the New Sith Wars. At that [[Time/Legends|time]], friends and families of the Jedi numbering in the thousands fled to form a refuge from [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] attacks. The group established a small colony on Yanibar, and once it was established, the colonists expected the Jedi to return for them once the wars had ended. They were not aware that every Jedi with knowledge of the refuge had [[Death/Legends|died]] in combat, taking the secret of the families and friends with them to their graves.<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> Due to Yanibar's seasonal extremes, most of the original settlers died in the first few [[Standard year|years]] after landing there.<ref name="JATM" />

Forgotten, the colonists eked out a living from the trying conditions of Yanibar. The Force abilities of many of the colonists were honed into the Zeison Sha tradition and contributed significantly to their survival, and they somewhat prospered. A few [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] after arriving on Yanibar, the planet was discovered by fringe [[Merchant/Legends|traders]], and a small [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] and outpost were established, although the organization and planet of Yanibar remained in relative obscurity.<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> They did establish relations with the traders, which gave the Zeison Sha some knowledge of [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] affairs.<ref name="JATM" />

===Galactic travelers and the outlanders===
{{Quote|Despite the Jedi Order's hope to reconcile, it seems that the Zeison Sha are determined to remain independent.|Jedi Master Bodo Baas, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="EGTTF" />}}
After the end of the wars, some Zeison Sha returned to the wider galaxy, only to find that the Jedi Order desired to take in their force Force-sensitive children for training and conversion into the Order. Angered, they returned to Yanibar with reports of Jedi child-snatching. The general view of the Jedi diminished significantly as a result. Still, some members left their world and journeyed abroad throughout the organization's history. They often did so incognito, especially after the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="EGTTF" /> Zeison Sha in the galaxy rarely stayed in one place. Instead, they traveled the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|spacelanes]] in search of those in need.<ref name="JATM" />

[[File:Bodo_Baas_color.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Jedi Master Bodo Baas visited the Zeison Sha in 580 BBY.]]
In [[580 BBY]], the Zeison Sha received a visit to Yanibar by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Bodo Baas]] on a diplomatic mission. Years of planning had gone into the mission as Baas hoped to convince the Zeison Sha to join forces with the Jedi. The Zeison Sha were suspicious of the Jedi and would not allow the Jedi unsupervised contact with their children. Baas found their philosophy on familial integrity alien and also expressed concern about their emphasis on self-sufficiency. One warrior, a young [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], told the Jedi delegation that if not for self-reliance, the original settlers would have perished after the ancestors of the Jedi left them there. Following the cold reception, the Jedi commission departed, realizing that the Zeison Sha were completely unwilling to entertain any idea of unification with the Jedi Order.<ref name="EGTTF" />

Despite the numerous dangers of the galaxy, the Zeison Sha survived during the reign of the Empire, although [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial troops]] did occupy Yanibar. The Zeison Sha did not take kindly to this, with members actively resisting the Imperial occupation.<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> The Zeison Sha ultimately survived the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], avoiding the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and his [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunters]] but remaining unreconciled to the Jedi even after the fall of the Empire. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Zeison Sha quietly celebrated the Jedi lost during the war but also wished the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] invaders would avoid Yanibar and leave the galaxy peacefully.<ref name="JATM" /> In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], their existence and history, including the prior encounter with Baas, were documented by Jedi Master and [[Jedi historian|historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]] in her compendium, ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]''.<ref name="EGTTF" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Zeison Sha were first mentioned in the [[2003]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' guidebook ''[[Hero's Guide]]'', written by [[Rodney Thompson]] and [[J.D. Wiker]]. Their depiction was illustrated by [[Wayne Reynolds]].<ref name="Hero's Guide" />

''Hero's Guide'' describes the Zeison Sha as first coming into existence "nearly two millennia before the rise of the Empire"—referring to the [[Declaration of a New Order|founding]] of the Galactic Empire<ref name="Hero's Guide" /> in 19 BBY.<ref name="EGTTF" /> This is conflicted by the appearance of Zeison Sha armor in the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'',<ref name="kotorII" /> which is set in 3951 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="TEA KOTORII date" /> The continuity error was expounded upon with the reference to the Zeison Sha living on Yanibar in the "Might of the Zeison Sha" crew skill mission in [[2014]]'s [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan|''Shadow of Revan'']] digital expansion to the video game ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'',<ref name="SWTOR" /> which is set in the 3600s BBY.<ref name="TOR dates">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' is set in the [[Galactic Standard Calendar|3600s BBY]] according to ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''.</ref> As such, this article assumes that the Zeison Sha indeed existed by 3951 BBY as established by ''The Sith Lords''.
